groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Topic, goal, audience, takeaway, facts, platform, duration, format, CTA, language.

groups.title: Platform & Production
groups.description: Filming method, available assets, production level, and variants.

groups.title: Brand & Style
groups.description: Tone, brand voice, pacing, and caption style.

groups.title: Accuracy & Restrictions
groups.description: Sources, restrictions, sensitive subject category, and jurisdiction.

variables.label: Video topic
variables.placeholder: Explain what the video should be about.
variables.hint: Describe the subject, product, story, question, or idea the video should cover.

help.why: The topic is the central factual and creative input that cannot be safely inferred.
help.example: One practical tip for clearer product demos on Reels
help.avoid: Several unrelated topics in a 30-second script.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main result you want.
variables.hint: Choose the single most important purpose of the video.

help.why: The goal determines the video structure, emphasis, CTA, and pacing.
help.avoid: Stacking sell + engage + entertain as equal goals.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Who should watch this, and what do they already know?
variables.hint: Describe the intended viewer, relevant interests, and awareness level.

help.why: Audience knowledge and motivation materially affect language, examples, and hook selection.
help.example: Who should watch this, and what do they already know?
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Main takeaway
variables.placeholder: What should viewers understand, remember, or believe?
variables.hint: State the one central message the video must communicate.

help.why: A defined takeaway prevents the short script from becoming unfocused.
help.avoid: Vague multi-message scripts.

variables.label: Facts to include
variables.placeholder: Add the facts, steps, features, dates, examples, or details that must appear.
variables.hint: Include only information that is accurate and approved for use.

help.why: The model must receive the factual details it is not permitted to invent.
help.example: Only verified steps, features, or dates
help.avoid: Invented stats, testimonials, or guarantees.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Select the script language.
variables.hint: The script, on-screen text, caption, and notes will use this language.

help.why: Language cannot always be reliably inferred from the topic or target platform.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Target platform
variables.placeholder: Choose where the video will be published.
variables.hint: Select one platform or use a broadly compatible vertical-video format.

help.why: The destination affects pacing, tone, CTA language, caption style, and publishing conventions.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Target duration
variables.placeholder: Choose the approximate duration.
variables.hint: The spoken word count and number of scenes will be adapted to this duration.

help.why: Duration directly limits the amount of spoken content and the number of visual beats.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Video format
variables.placeholder: Select a format or let the model decide.
variables.hint: Choose the presentation structure that best fits your idea.

help.why: Different video formats require different narrative and scene structures.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Viewer action
variables.placeholder: Choose what viewers should do next.
variables.hint: Use one clear action or select no CTA for a purely informational ending.

help.why: The desired action determines how the final beat should conclude.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Offer details
variables.placeholder: Describe the product, service, price, offer, destination, and approved claims.
variables.hint: Include only confirmed information that may appear in the promotional script.

help.why: Commercial scripts require accurate offer information that must not be fabricated.
help.example: Confirmed product name, price, and terms only
help.avoid: Fake discounts or scarcity.

variables.label: Platform name
variables.placeholder: Enter the platform or publishing destination.
variables.hint: Add the platform name and any important format requirement you know.

help.why: A custom destination may have conventions that differ from the supported platform presets.
help.example: Enter the platform or publishing destination.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Delivery method
variables.placeholder: How will the message be presented?
variables.hint: The script and visual directions will match the selected recording style.

help.why: Spoken delivery and scene instructions differ substantially between recording methods.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Available visuals
variables.placeholder:
Creator on camera
Product footage
Screen recording
Existing photos
Existing video clips
Simple B-roll
Stock footage
Text and graphics only
variables.hint: The script will avoid requesting footage or assets that are unavailable. Select all that apply (one per line). Options: Creator on camera; Product footage; Screen recording; Existing photos; Existing video clips; Simple B-roll; Stock footage; Text and graphics only.

help.why: Visual directions must remain practical for the creator’s available assets.
help.avoid: Requesting footage you cannot shoot.

variables.label: Production level
variables.placeholder: Choose the realistic editing level.
variables.hint: Controls shot complexity, transitions, effects, and editing instructions.

help.why: The production level prevents unrealistic filming or editing recommendations.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Number of versions
variables.hint: Generate one to three meaningfully different script versions.

help.why: The user may need alternative concepts without receiving an excessive number of versions.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Select the desired tone.
variables.hint: Choose the overall emotional and verbal style.

help.why: Tone materially changes word choice, hook intensity, delivery, and caption style.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Brand voice notes
variables.placeholder: Add preferred wording, personality, phrases to avoid, or a short style sample.
variables.hint: Leave blank to use a natural neutral voice suited to the audience.

help.why: Existing brand language cannot be inferred reliably from a general tone selection.
help.example: Add preferred wording, personality, phrases to avoid, or a short style sample.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Pacing
variables.placeholder: Choose the speed and rhythm.
variables.hint: Controls sentence length, cut frequency, pauses, and information density.

help.why: Pacing affects both the spoken script and the editing rhythm.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Caption style
variables.placeholder: Choose the accompanying post style.
variables.hint: Controls caption length, CTA repetition, emoji use, and hashtag suggestions.

help.why: The publishing caption serves a different function from the spoken script and may require separate treatment.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Sources or references
variables.placeholder: Paste approved notes, quotations, URLs, source excerpts, or product documentation.
variables.hint: Use this for claims that must follow a specific source or approved wording.

help.why: Source material supports accurate claims and reduces the risk of invented information.
help.example: Paste approved notes, quotations, URLs, source excerpts, or product documentation.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Restrictions
variables.placeholder: List prohibited claims, sensitive details, required wording, or topics to avoid.
variables.hint: Combine all special limitations and mandatory requirements here.

help.why: User-specific legal, brand, privacy, or factual restrictions cannot always be inferred.
help.example: List prohibited claims, sensitive details, required wording, or topics to avoid.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.

variables.label: Sensitive subject
variables.placeholder: Select a regulated category when relevant.
variables.hint: This enables additional caution for subjects where inaccurate claims may cause harm.

help.why: High-stakes subjects require stricter wording and verification safeguards.
help.avoid: Medical/legal claims without approved wording.

variables.label: Country or jurisdiction
variables.placeholder: For example: Ukraine, United States, EU, California.
variables.hint: Add the relevant location when laws, rights, benefits, or regulations may differ.

help.why: Legal, financial, political, employment, tax, insurance, and regulated claims may depend on jurisdiction.
help.example: For example: Ukraine, United States, EU, California.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, engagement bait, or unsafe claims.
